repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: sharp ADE bounds from one shared hidden-graph completion under treatment-exposure misspecification; parent similarity exempt in upgrade mode

published_prior_art.cite: hoshino_yanagi_2024_noncompliance_unknown_interference identifies/estimates ITT and complier effects under unknown interference; schroder_oprescu_feuerriegel_kallus_2026_networks_partialid gives sharp exposure-misspecification bounds without IV compliance

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1: shared-edge compatibility makes cellwise bounds nonsharp; parent similarity exempt, and this adds IV compliance plus dual exposure channels

published_prior_art.cite: vazquez_bare_2023_iv_spillovers shows IV spillover contrasts aggregate compliance-type effects, but not shared-completion sharp gaps

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2: joint projection logic for dynamic IV bounds; no network-completion object

published_prior_art.cite: imai_jiang_malani_2021_interference_noncompliance identifies complier direct/spillover effects; nibbering_oosterveen_2025_iv_interference_policy partially identifies policy-relevant IV effects with interference

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship strict-gap claim cites Savje and Vazquez-Bare without a section/equation/page anchor to the specific gap being closed.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The open-set nonrectangular joint-family claim has no concrete non-corner worked instance computing the joint region versus the stitched rectangle.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal promises a joint sharp family from one polytope but Exhibit 9.1 computes only a scalar direct-effect gap, not the joint vector or stitched-rectangle failure; tier=field below novelty_target=flagship.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The stated polytope has outcome dimension [0,1]^{2n|S|}, but Section 6 defines Y_i(d,s,r_D), which requires the hidden-exposure factor; the observed-law matching also omits full joint D/S/W constraints needed for sharpness relative to P.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Exhibit 9.1 claims the shared-completion upper endpoint is 1/2, but the feasible hidden-edge vectors include (0,0), which lets both leaves avoid hidden instrument exposure and destroys the claimed strict gap.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The proof sketch only says to project the same feasible set and compare with the rectangle; it gives no concrete algebraic route or witness for strict nonrectangularity on an open set.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair Exhibit 9.1 by adding a non-circular primitive restriction or observable moment condition that rules out the empty hidden graph, then recompute both product and shared-completion endpoints.
Restate Theorem 1 with the correct latent outcome dimension and full observable-law matching constraints for D, S, W, and Y, not only unitwise take-up and conditional outcome means.
Add a separate worked instance for Conjecture 2 that computes the joint identified region and the coordinatewise stitched rectangle on the same finite network.

Add location-level anchors for the flagship comparators, especially the exact Hoshino-Yanagi, Vazquez-Bare, Imai-Jiang-Malani, and Nibbering-Oosterveen results that the proposal claims not to subsume it.

recommended_next_step: Reject this version; redraft the finite witness and sharpness theorem before another flagship upgrade review.

upgrade_review.upgrade_delta_one_line: The proposal does widen the parent from treatment-equals-assignment exposure misspecification to latent monotone IV take-up with dual exposure channels and includes a parent-corner reduction, although the flagship witness is currently unsound.
